elements.title: a townhouse
elements.contents: ... that the voivode of a Polish region bought a townhouse (pictured) for his brother, an abbot who converted it to an inn?

elements.title: Liz Pelly
elements.contents: ... that in 2025, Liz Pelly published the book Mood Machine, a critical examination of Spotify including the platform's promotion of fake artists?

elements.title: Aeao
elements.contents: ... that "Aeao" was involved in a multi-platform trend joined by numerous K-pop celebrities nine years after its release?

elements.title: Viktor Pylypenko
elements.contents: ... that Viktor Pylypenko ended a relationship with his Emirati boyfriend and returned to Ukraine to fight in the Russo-Ukrainian war?

elements.title: Ottoman Iraq
elements.contents: ... that Ottoman Iraq's nationality law was a legal basis for Saddam Hussein to classify up to two million Iraqis as "inauthentic" and expel as many as 400,000 of them?

elements.title: Dominic Sessa
elements.contents: ... that actor Dominic Sessa made his film debut with a breakthrough role as a student in the Christmas film The Holdovers?

elements.title: Boone and Scenic Valley Railroad 8419
elements.contents: ... that Boone and Scenic Valley Railroad 8419 was one of the first Chinese steam locomotives to be exported to the United States?

elements.title: opposed devolution
elements.contents: ... that Scottish politician Tam Dalyell opposed devolution, warning it would become a "motorway without exit to a separate state"?

elements.title: Agnes Borinsky
elements.contents: ... that, in Agnes Borinsky's queer interpretation of the Bible's Song of Songs, audience members placed offerings on a "shrine to the dead"?
